items.description: The Iowa Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) website outlines the state agency’s role in providing programs and services that protect and improve the health and well-being of individuals, families, and communities across Iowa. The department oversees a wide range of services, including behavioral health, disability services, public health initiatives, and social support programs, with a focus on promoting resilience and access to care. A key initiative highlighted on the site is the development of Iowa’s Behavioral Health Service System, which integrates mental health and substance use services into a more coordinated, statewide system designed to improve accessibility and ensure that individuals can receive care regardless of where they enter the system. Overall, the website emphasizes a person-centered approach that prioritizes prevention, treatment, recovery, and crisis support for all Iowans
items.title: Iowa Department of Health and Human Services
Pennsylvania  Department of Drug and Alcohol Programs
Pennsylvania  Office of Mental Health and Substance Abuse Services
South Carolina Department of Behavioral Health and Developmental Disabilities, Office of Substance Use Services

items.description: DBHDD operates state hospitals and provides for community-based services across the state through contracted providers. The department serves people living with mental health challenges, substance use disorders, intellectual and developmental disabilities, or any combination of these. As Georgia’s public safety net, our primary responsibility is to serve people who are uninsured. We also serve individuals on Medicaid and others with few resources or options. Our program divisions are: Behavioral Health, Developmental Disabilities, Hospital Services.
items.title: Georgia Department of Behavioral Health and Developmental Disabilities
Texas Health and Human Services Commission

items.description: The Kentucky Cabinet for Health and Family Services (CHFS) website outlines a state agency responsible for administering a wide range of health and human services programs aimed at improving the well-being of Kentucky residents. The agency oversees key areas such as Medicaid, public health, behavioral health, child welfare, and family support services, working through multiple departments and community partnerships to deliver care and assistance. CHFS provides access to essential services including healthcare coverage, crisis support, and public assistance programs, while also emphasizing prevention, recovery, and community well-being. Overall, the website reflects a comprehensive, statewide system designed to support vulnerable populations, promote health outcomes, and ensure access to necessary services across Kentucky
items.title: Kentucky Cabinet for Health and Family Services
Utah Department of Health and Human Services, Office of Substance Use and Mental Health
South Dakota Department of Social Services

items.description: The Alcohol & Drug Abuse Division is the primary and often sole source of public funds for substance abuse treatment. ADAD’s treatment efforts are designed to promote a statewide, culturally appropriate, comprehensive system of services to meet the treatment and recovery needs of individuals and families. As a requirement, treatment services prioritize admission for pregnant women and injection drug users.
items.title:
Hawaii Department of Health
Alcohol and Drug Abuse Division

items.description: NJRC offers comprehensive wraparound services, including addiction treatment and medication-assisted treatment MAT; medical, dental, and psychiatric healthcare; federal, state, and county social services; case management; employment assistance and job training; legal services bail and court fine dismissal, birth certificate, drivers license, and social security card restoration, expungements; and veteran-specific support, discharge upgrades, HUD-VASH housing, and VA services.
items.title: The New Jersey Reentry Corporation
Rhode Island Department of Behavioral Healthcare, Developmental Disabilities & Hospitals

items.description: Welcome to the Division of Substance Abuse and Mental Health Providers Resources Home.  Each area in the grid below includes selected resources and required information by topic for prospective or current DSAMH affiliated Behavioral Health service providers.  Each area has an introduction describing the resources available at the top of each section.  We strongly recommend prospective and current DSAMH affiliated providers peruse each section’s introductory description for an overall orientation to these resources.
items.title: Division of Substance Abuse and Mental Health

items.description: Arizona Health Care Cost Containment System is Arizona's Medicaid agency that offers health care programs to serve Arizona residents. Individuals must meet certain income and other requirements to obtain services.
items.title: Arizona Health Care Cost Containment System

items.description: The Connecticut Department of Mental Health and Addiction Services is a health care agency whose mission is to promote the overall health and wellness of persons with behavioral health needs through an integrated network of holistic, comprehensive, effective, and efficient services and supports that foster dignity, respect, and self-sufficiency in those we serve.
items.title: Connecticut Department of Mental Health and Addiction Services
Tennessee Department of Mental Health and Substance Abuse Services
North Dakota Department of Health and Human Services

items.description: Find out about the different programs and services offered through DHCS for individuals and stakeholders.  Find out how a physician can enroll you in Medi-Cal, how to receive free health screenings, and family planning services. Get help for children in foster care, children with special medical conditions and seniors needing personal care.
items.title: California Department of Health Care Services

items.description: The Alabama Department of Mental Health serves more than 200,000 Alabama citizens with mental illnesses, developmental disabilities, and substance use disorders. Our mission is to Serve, Empower, and Support, and promote the health and well-being of Alabamians.
items.title: Alabama Department of Mental Health

items.description: The Division of Behavioral Health has a commitment to improve the quality of life of Alaskans through the right service to the right person at the right time. The central purpose of the division is to promote an integrated and comprehensive behavioral health system based on sound policy, effective practices, and partnerships.
items.title: Alaska Department of Health

items.description:
The Behavioral Health Services Commission is the state mental health authority and is responsible for managing mental health funding in Kansas. In addition, it facilitates addiction and prevention services and programs for the State of Kansas, including targeted workforce development initiatives. In addition, the commission works in close collaboration with the Governor’s Behavioral Health Services Planning Council.

Our work is supported by the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration, and KDADS. KDADS is responsible, by statute, and holds the authority and responsibility to coordinate and provide substance abuse and mental health services in Kansas. We promote effective public policy and develop and evaluate programs and resources for behavioral health prevention, treatment, and recovery services.
items.title: Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services

items.description: Together we improve the quality of life of all Arkansans by protecting the vulnerable, fostering independence, and promoting better health.
items.title: Arkansas Department of Human Services

items.description: Domestic Violence, / safety resource hub; ask about emergency shelter pathways and eligibility.
items.title: Heartline

items.description: The Illinois Department of Human Services (IDHS) website provides an overview of a state agency responsible for delivering a wide range of social and health services to residents, particularly those experiencing economic hardship, disabilities, or behavioral health needs. The site highlights the department’s role in overseeing multiple divisions, including mental health, substance use prevention and recovery, developmental disabilities, and rehabilitation services. It emphasizes a coordinated, statewide system designed to promote health, safety, and independence through prevention, treatment, and support programs. Additionally, IDHS focuses on expanding access to community-based services, crisis intervention, and recovery-oriented care, ensuring individuals across Illinois can receive comprehensive and culturally responsive support
items.title: Illinois Department of Human Services

items.description: The Louisiana Department of Health’s Office of Behavioral Health (OBH) webpage outlines the state’s system for delivering mental health and substance use services to residents. OBH is responsible for managing and coordinating a comprehensive network of care that includes community-based treatment programs, inpatient psychiatric services, and prevention initiatives aimed at improving overall quality of life. The agency emphasizes recovery, resilience, and accessibility by ensuring services are available to individuals with mental illness and addictive disorders across Louisiana. It works collaboratively with partners to provide culturally competent, integrated care that addresses both behavioral health and broader social support needs. Additionally, the site highlights crisis resources, including the 988 lifeline and other support lines, reinforcing the state’s commitment to timely intervention and ongoing support
items.title: Louisiana Department of Health’s Office of Behavioral Health

items.description: The Hawaiʻi Department of Health’s Adult Mental Health Division (AMHD) website outlines a statewide system designed to provide comprehensive mental health services for adults experiencing serious mental illness. The division focuses on promoting recovery, independence, and overall well-being through culturally relevant and community-based care. AMHD coordinates a wide range of services, including case management, housing support, outpatient treatment, and inpatient psychiatric care, while working in partnership with community providers and other healthcare systems. The site also highlights crisis intervention resources such as the 988 crisis line, which offers 24/7 support for individuals in mental health emergencies. Overall, the division emphasizes accessibility, recovery-oriented care, and integrated services that support individuals in living independently and improving their quality of life
items.title: Hawaii Department of Health Adult Mental Health Division

items.description: Find treatment facilities in the United States or U.S. Territories for: substance abuse, addiction, mental health problems.
items.title: FindTreatment.gov

items.description: Find mental health or substance use treatment services, access contact details for helplines or crisis lines where individuals can seek immediate assistance or guidance, and learn about support resources for individuals in recovery, including tips, tools, and support networks.
items.title: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration

items.description: Transitional Services options across Oklahoma that may work with people on probation, parole, or with a record
items.title: Homeless Alliance
South Carolina Department of Behavioral Health and Developmental Disabilities, Office of Mental Health

items.title: Homeless Alliance Resources

items.description: The Indiana Division of Mental Health and Addiction (DMHA), part of the Family and Social Services Administration, is responsible for overseeing and regulating mental health and substance use services across the state. The agency establishes care standards, certifies providers, and ensures that individuals have access to quality, recovery-oriented services that promote resilience for individuals, families, and communities. DMHA works through a statewide network of community mental health centers, addiction treatment providers, and certified behavioral health clinics, while also operating state psychiatric hospitals for more intensive care needs. In addition to oversight and service coordination, the division supports prevention, treatment, recovery, and crisis intervention programs, emphasizing accessibility and comprehensive care for diverse populations throughout Indiana
items.title: Indiana Division of Mental Health and Addiction (DMHA)

items.description: The Behavioral Health Administration is the state administration responsible for ensuring all people in Colorado have access to quality mental health and substance use disorder services, regardless of where they live, or ability to pay. BHA will help bring together community groups and governmental agencies to design a behavioral health system for all people in Colorado that is easy to access, and offers high-quality care that considers the whole person and their needs.
items.title: Colorado Behavioral Health Administration

items.description: The Idaho Department of Health and Welfare’s Behavioral Health page provides an overview of the state’s efforts to support individuals experiencing mental health and substance use disorders. The site emphasizes a mission centered on promoting hope, recovery, and resilience through a coordinated system of care. It outlines key service areas, including adult behavioral health services, children’s behavioral health programs, substance use disorder treatment, and crisis intervention resources. The page also highlights the availability of opioid education and prevention tools, as well as 24/7 crisis support options. Overall, the resource presents behavioral health care as accessible, treatable, and supported through a statewide network of programs designed to meet the diverse needs of Idaho residents
items.title: Behavioral Health
Oklahoma Department of Mental Health and Substance Abuse Services

items.title: Community Action Partnership
North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services
